milestone: A2 (A-b): in-place COW checkpoint commit - increment 2b (non-empty file / data extent)
status: Passed (unit + host reader round-trip + Apple kernel reads file content + Apple fsck_apfs clean, no warnings). A2 file-insert capability certified for files with content.
capability: commitImageOnlyFileInsert now writes a file payload: it allocates data blocks past the COW chain, writes the (zero-padded) payload, emits the j_file_extent record, copy-on-writes the extent-ref tree (a seventh chain block) with a j_phys_ext record per data extent, points the COW'd volume superblock at it, and accounts the net allocation across the volume apfs_fs_alloc_count + the re-emitted spaceman device free count + the chunk-info-block chunk-0 free count.
code: src/core/partition_apfs_writer.cpp: writeApfsFileDataBlocks, adjustCibFreeCount, applyFileInsertAllocation, cowExtentRefTree (reuses buildExtentRefTreeBlock), spacemanFreeDelta threaded through reemitCheckpointEphemerals/advanceCheckpoint, walkLiveFsChain extended to read the volume extent-ref tree oid.
fix_history: First non-empty fsck flagged two warnings: apfs_fs_alloc_count expected 5 / actual 6, and overallocation on the data block (0xcf). Root cause: the data block was not registered in the extent-ref tree, from which fsck credits the volume's space.
Fix: copy-on-write the extent-ref tree with the data extent's j_phys_ext record; re-run fsck -> fully clean.
unit_test: tests/unit/test_partition_manager_core.cpp::apfsWriter_inPlaceFileInsertCommitAddsReadableFile - inserts a 40-byte file, asserts the reader reports the exact size, the payload lands in the data block past the seven COW chain blocks (block 208), the extent-ref tree block is checksum-valid, and the spaceman device free count drops by one. 115/115 core subtests pass.

vm_external_gate.environment: qemu/KVM macOS Sequoia recovery; apfs.kext 2332.101.1; in-place file-insert container with a 62-byte file (xid 3) attached raw as SATA disk0
method: 1. format-image (A2NZ, xid 2) -> commit-image-file-insert --file-name hello.txt --payload-file (62 bytes) (xid 3), host-verified (reader size 62; extent-ref phys_ext record).

2. Attached to the macOS VM. Apple kernel auto-mounted /Volumes/A2NZ read-write; `cat /Volumes/A2NZ/hello.txt` printed the exact file content - apfs.kext read the COW-inserted file's data extent (data block 208).
3. apfs.kext continued the S.A.K. checkpoint ring (xid 3 -> 5).
vm_external_gate.kernel_data_read: cat /Volumes/A2NZ/hello.txt -> 'Hello from S.A.K. in-place COW file insert with real content!' (full 62-byte payload read from the data extent)
vm_external_gate.apple_fsck_apfs: Checking ... fsroot tree / extent ref tree / volume object map space / allocated space all consistent (no apfs_fs_alloc_count or overallocation warnings). The volume A2NZ appears to be OK. The container /dev/disk0 appears to be OK.
vm_external_gate.evidence_png: a2nz3-cat.png (kernel reads the content) + a2nz3-fsck.png (clean fsck); a2nz-fsck2.png shows the pre-fix alloc_count + overallocation warnings

gates: clang-format + lizard (CCN<=10, params<=5) + cppcheck clean; commits ad80ed4 (engine) + dd3d1e4 (extent-ref tree), all pre-commit hooks passed, no bypass.
next: Chained commits (walk past the first COW for existing files), multi-block files, free-queue crash-safety (defer freeing old chain blocks), arbitrary Apple containers. A-b full flip then needs physical-USB destructive + crash + rollback like A1/H1.
